item.title: [UPDATED] "Miasma" Worm Spreads Through npm Using Novel "Phantom Gyp" Evasion Technique
item.description:
A sophisticated, self-propagating worm named 'Miasma' is actively spreading through the npm open-source registry, using a novel evasion technique dubbed 'Phantom Gyp.' The attack bypasses typical security checks by abusing 'binding.gyp' files to execute malicious code during the 'npm install' proces...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Sygnia: Lone Actor Used AI as 'Force Multiplier' to Accelerate Cloud Attack
item.description:
Cyber readiness firm Sygnia is investigating a sophisticated, financially motivated attack where a single threat actor used AI as a 'force multiplier' to compromise a global enterprise's cloud environment at unprecedented speed. The AI-assisted attack executed hundreds of unique SQL queries in paral...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Intelligence, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Government agencies hit by ransomware daily in H1 2026, study finds
item.description:
A new study from Comparitech reveals a troubling trend: government organizations were successfully attacked by ransomware at an average rate of one per day in the first half of 2026. The 187 documented attacks mark a 13% increase from the latter half of 2025. The United States was the most frequent ...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Ransomware Attacks in Europe Skyrocket by 55% as Supply Chains Become Prime Targets
item.description:
A new report from cyber risk management firm Black Kite reveals a staggering 55.1% year-over-year increase in ransomware attacks across Europe in the first four months of 2026. The manufacturing sector was the most heavily targeted industry. Five countries—Germany, the UK, France, Italy, and Spain—a...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] New 'LegacyHive' Windows Zero-Day Exploit Grants Admin Access
item.description:
A security researcher has publicly disclosed 'LegacyHive,' a new zero-day privilege escalation exploit affecting fully patched Microsoft Windows systems. The proof-of-concept (PoC) abuses a flaw in the Windows User Profile Service to gain read access to another user's data, including application set...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Inc Ransomware Exploits Critical SonicWall SMA Zero-Day Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Two critical zero-day vulnerabilities in SonicWall's Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series appliances are being actively exploited by the Inc Ransomware group and another threat actor, UTA0533. The chained flaws (CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410) allow unauthenticated attackers to achieve remote c...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] AI-Powered Ransomware 'JadePuffer' Deploys 'ENCFORGE' to Destroy ML Models
item.description:
The autonomous AI threat actor JadePuffer has resurfaced, deploying a new ransomware payload named ENCFORGE. This malware is specifically designed to find and destroy AI and machine learning (ML) models, targeting over 180 file extensions common in the ML development stack. The attack, which involve...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Healthcare Software Firm Craneware Hit by Data Breach, Data Stolen
item.description:
Craneware, a UK-based software supplier for over 2,000 US hospitals, has confirmed it suffered a cyberattack resulting in a significant data breach. The company announced on July 20, 2026, that an unauthorized third party exfiltrated a 'significant volume' of data, including records belonging to emp...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Cyberattack, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Ransomware attack on Coca-Cola's Fairlife halts U.S. milk production
item.description:
Fairlife, a dairy subsidiary of The Coca-Cola Company, has suspended all U.S. production after a ransomware attack compromised its IT and operational technology networks. The breach was detected on July 16, 2026, leading to an immediate shutdown of affected systems and the launch of an incident resp...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] JADEPUFFER: The First Fully Autonomous AI-Driven Ransomware Attack is Here
item.description:
Security researchers have documented a paradigm-shifting cyberattack conducted by 'JADEPUFFER,' the first observed fully autonomous, AI-driven ransomware. Unlike traditional ransomware operated by humans, this 'Agentic Threat Actor' (ATA) independently breached a target by exploiting CVE-2025-3248 i...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Malware, Threat Actor, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] SonicWall Warns of Two Zero-Days in SMA 1000 Under Active Exploit
item.description:
SonicWall has released urgent security patches for two zero-day vulnerabilities, CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, affecting its SMA 1000 series appliances. Threat actors are chaining the flaws—a server-side request forgery (SSRF) and a code injection bug—to achieve unauthenticated remote code exec...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Identity Attacks Now Top Ransomware Vector, Eclipsing Exploits
item.description:
According to the Sophos 'State of Ransomware 2026' report, compromised identities are now the primary root cause of ransomware attacks, accounting for 79% of incidents. For the first time in four years, malicious email and phishing have surpassed software vulnerability exploitation as the top initia...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Ransomware attacks up 20% year-over-year in H1 2026, says report
item.description:
A new report from NordStellar indicates that ransomware attacks increased by 20% in the first half of 2026 compared to the same period in 2025, with 5,257 incidents recorded. While Q2 2026 saw a slight 4% dip from Q1, the attack volume remains at a high baseline of over 2,500 incidents per quarter. ...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Critical Unauthenticated RCE Flaw Found in WordPress Core
item.description:
A critical unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability, CVE-2026-63030, has been discovered in WordPress Core. The flaw, which affects versions 6.9.0 through 6.9.4 and 7.0.0 through 7.0.1, allows an attacker to gain complete control of a website via the REST API. WordPress has released...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Ernst & Young Discloses Data Breach via Third-Party Support System
item.description:
Global professional services firm Ernst & Young (EY) has disclosed a data breach that occurred via a compromised third-party IT service management platform. Between March and April 2026, an unauthorized actor gained access to the system and downloaded documents containing sensitive personal and fina...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Supply Chain Attack, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Tata Electronics Suffers Data Breach; Apple and Tesla Supplier Data Leaked
item.description:
Indian electronics manufacturer Tata Electronics, a key supplier for Apple and Tesla, has confirmed a cyberattack after the 'World Leaks' ransomware group claimed to have stolen 630GB of data. The leaked files, now on the dark web, allegedly include sensitive documents such as component designs for ...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Supply Chain Attack, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] CISA Adds Actively Exploited SharePoint RCE Flaw to KEV Catalog, Mandates Urgent Patching
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a high-severity remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint Server, tracked as CVE-2026-45659, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. Citing evidence of active in-the-wild exploitation, CI...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] New 'LegacyHive' Windows Zero-Day Exploit Published After Patch Tuesday
item.description:
A security researcher known as NightmareEclipse has publicly disclosed 'LegacyHive,' a new zero-day vulnerability affecting fully patched Windows systems, just one day after Microsoft's July 2026 Patch Tuesday. The researcher released a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit that abuses path resolution in t...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] New Email Attacks Use Real Microsoft Login Pages to Bypass MFA
item.description:
Security researchers are warning about increasingly sophisticated email attacks, including the Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform. This adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) attack uses a reverse proxy to present the victim with a real Microsoft login page. When the user authenticates and co...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Critical Oracle E-Business Suite RCE Flaw (CVE-2026-46817) Under Active Attack
item.description:
A critical, unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS), tracked as CVE-2026-46817, is being actively exploited in the wild. The flaw, which holds a CVSS score of 9.8, affects the Oracle Payments component and allows a remote attacker with network acces...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Patch Management

Read Full Article →
